title: OMA Harness DSL (v1/v2)
description: Single-source YAML surface for an OMA plugin. oma-compile reads this and emits .mcp.json and kiro-agents/<agent>.agent.json. Native runtimes (Claude Code marketplace, Kiro) consume the emitted files unchanged. v2 adds optional workflows (DAG), telemetry, policies, and metadata sections without touching the emission contract.

version.description: DSL schema version. v1 = the original contract. v2 adds optional workflows/telemetry/policies/metadata blocks; v1 files continue to compile unchanged.

metadata.description: (v2 only) Optional Kubernetes-style metadata block. Used by harness consumers for labelling/filtering; the compiler itself ignores it.

workflows.description: (v2 only) Named workflow DAGs. Each step refers to an already-declared agent or skill. The compiler validates the DAG (no cycles, step refs resolve) but does not execute it; runtime wiring lands in a later release.

telemetry.description: (v2 only) OpenTelemetry Collector wiring. Consumers (autopilot, agenticops) emit spans/metrics/logs to these endpoints; the compiler only validates the shape.

policies.description: (v2 only) Declarative runtime-enforcement rules. Each entry carries an `enforce` block the compiler translates into hooks/harness-rules.json plus a PreToolUse entry in hooks/hooks.json, enforced by the bundled enforce.py before a tool runs. Pure Claude Code; no external policy engine.

plugin.description: Plugin id. Must match the directory name under plugins/.

mcp.description: MCP server definitions keyed by short id. Short ids are what agents.mcp[] references.

hooks.description: Harness hooks. Keys are Claude Code hook event names (session-start, pre-tool-use, ...).

triggers.description: Keyword triggers merged into .omao/triggers.json at compile time.

mcp.description: Short ids into the top-level mcp: map. Unknown ids are rejected by the compiler.

welcomeMessage.description: Kiro-specific welcome text. Passed through when runtime=kiro.

tools.description: Kiro tools allowlist. Passed through when runtime=kiro.

resources.description: Kiro resources list (file://, skill://...). Passed through when runtime=kiro.

notes.description: Free-form metadata block copied under `_meta` on the emitted agent.json.

mcpEnvOverrides.description: Per-mcp-id env overrides applied ONLY to the emitted Kiro agent.json (not the Claude Code .mcp.json).

args.description: Must contain a pinned version (==X.Y.Z). Compiler rejects floating versions.

runs.description: Path to the shell script, relative to the plugin root.

policyRef.description: (v2 only) A declarative runtime-enforcement rule. The compiler emits these into hooks/harness-rules.json plus a PreToolUse entry in hooks/hooks.json, so the bundled enforcer (tools/oma_harness/enforce.py) blocks matching tool calls before they run — pure Claude Code, no external policy engine.

enforce.description: Runtime deny rule evaluated by the PreToolUse hook. Fires when (tool matches or is omitted) AND every declared deny_if condition matches.

tool.description: Exact Claude Code tool name to match (e.g. Bash, Write, Edit). Omit to match any tool.

deny_if.description: Conditions that must all match for the rule to fire. At least one condition key is required.

decision.description: What the PreToolUse hook returns when the rule fires.

reason.description: Human-readable reason shown to the model when the call is blocked.

workflow.description: (v2 only) A named DAG of steps referencing declared agents or skills.

agent_ref.description: Id of an agent declared at agents[].id.

skill_ref.description: Id of a skill (resolved by the harness; not validated against the plugin file alone).

depends_on.description: Step ids in the same workflow that must complete first.

on_failure.description: How the harness should react if this step fails.

trigger.description: Keyword trigger descriptor compiled into .omao/triggers.json. The workspace hook hooks/user-prompt-submit.sh reads the emitted file to decide whether to surface a Tier-0 command suggestion.

id.description: Stable identifier used in hook logs.

keywords.description: Case-insensitive keywords or multi-word phrases. Any single match (with word boundaries for single tokens, substring for multi-word) fires the trigger.

context_required.description: Optional additional substrings that must all appear in the user's prompt before the trigger fires. Skipped when the user invokes an explicit slash command.

command.description: Slash command or skill id the keyword resolves to.

description.description: Human-readable one-liner surfaced to the agent when the trigger fires.
